tasks.name: Revise title and abstract
tasks.description: Rewrite title and abstract to lead with 'optimal compression' framing instead of graph predictions. Title: 'Feature Absorption as Optimal Compression: Evidence that Training Reduces Structural Artifacts'. Abstract must: (1) lead with null results (absorption does NOT degrade steering/probing), (2) include trained<random finding (8x lower), (3) frame as rate-distortion optimal behavior.

tasks.name: Revise background section
tasks.description: Restructure Section 2 (Background): (1) SAE basics, (2) feature absorption phenomenon (Chanin 2024), (3) rate-distortion theory perspective, (4) LCA neuroscience connection as background context, NOT as predictive framework. Be clear that graph predictions are tested later and falsified.

tasks.name: Revise theoretical framework section
tasks.description: Restructure Section 3 (Framework) to present LCA-SAE correspondence as mechanistic explanation, not predictive tool. Clearly state: we hypothesize that absorption is rate-distortion optimal compression behavior. Present the theoretical prediction but acknowledge it will be tested.

tasks.name: Revise H1-H4 results section
tasks.description: Update Section 4 (Results) for H1-H4: present null results with full statistics. Key: zero hypotheses survive MCP (12 tests, Bonferroni alpha=0.00417). H1b (delta-corrected) shows r=-0.431, p=0.028 uncorrected but does NOT survive correction.

visualizations.description: Hypothesis testing summary with corrected p-values

tasks.name: Revise H5-H6 results section
tasks.description: Update H5 (precision-recall asymmetry) and H6 (inhibition graph) results. H5: precision=1.0 universally at k>=5, recall varies - this is the ONE robust finding. H6: MUST clearly state falsification - precision@20=0.0, enrichment=0.0x, Fisher p=1.0. Graph does NOT predict absorption pairs.

tasks.name: Revise H7-H10 results section
tasks.description: Update H7 (trained<random) and H10 (random baseline validation) results. Key empirical contribution: trained SAE mean=0.034 vs random SAE mean=0.278 (8x difference), p<0.001. This is the main positive empirical finding supporting the optimal compression framing.

visualizations.description: Trained vs Random SAE absorption distribution

tasks.name: Revise discussion section
tasks.description: Rewrite Section 5 (Discussion): (1) Absorption as optimal compression interpretation, (2) Why graph predictions failed (decoder correlations capture general similarity, not specific parent-child relationships), (3) Why prior work found null results, (4) Relationship to Matryoshka/OrtSAE. REMOVE contradictory claim about graph identifying at-risk features.

tasks.name: Verify all data claims against source files
tasks.description: Cross-check every numerical claim in the paper against exp/results/full/ JSON files. Flag any claims without verifiable source (especially H8 'r=+0.12' claim about total incoming inhibition). Either find/compute the data or remove the unsupported claim.

tasks.name: Fix figure references and generate missing figures
tasks.description: Fix figure filename mismatches (text references fig7 but calls it Figure 2). Verify all referenced figures exist. Generate any missing figures: (1) LCA-SAE correspondence diagram, (2) precision-recall asymmetry plot, (3) random vs trained SAE comparison. Save to writing/figures/.

tasks.name: Integrate all sections into complete paper
tasks.description: Combine all revised sections into writing/paper.md. Ensure consistent terminology, cross-references, and flow. Update LaTeX in writing/latex/main.tex. Compile to PDF and verify structure.

tasks.name: Final review and quality check
tasks.description: Review integrated paper for: (1) all claims backed by evidence, (2) no contradictory statements, (3) consistent terminology, (4) figures properly referenced, (5) score target >7/10. Prepare final submission package.
